title: alchemilla colorata subsp. lapeyrousii
intro: Alchemilla colorata subsp. lapeyrousii, also known as Alpine lady's mantle, is a flowering plant species within the genus Alchemilla. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, particularly its anti-inflammatory and astringent effects, which can be attributed to the presence of tannins and flavonoids. The health benefits of Alpine lady's mantle include soothing skin irritations, reducing menstrual cramps, and providing antioxidant properties. Alpine lady's mantle can be used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, although its use should be guided by a qualified practitioner due to potential interactions and side effects.
